keyTerms.architectural language: The idea that buildings communicate through materials, proportions, and spatial organization rather than words
keyTerms.pointed arch: A Gothic architectural element that directs weight downward more efficiently than round arches, enabling taller structures
keyTerms.Gothic verticality: The upward-reaching design of Gothic cathedrals intended to evoke spiritual aspiration and divine transcendence
keyTerms.fascist architecture: Oversized neoclassical buildings used by authoritarian regimes to project permanence and legitimacy
keyTerms.neuroaesthetics: The scientific study of how built environments affect brain activity, emotion, and physiological state
